pattern: A binary reflected Gray code lists every n-bit value once so that successive entries differ in a single bit, and entry i is generated directly as i exclusive-or i-shifted-right-by-one.
hints: The goal is an ordering of all 2^n values where each step flips exactly one bit and the sequence wraps around at the ends.
Such a sequence can be built reflectively: take the (n-1)-bit code, then append its reverse with the new high bit set.
Equivalently and far simpler, the i-th value of the standard reflected code is i XOR (i >> 1); iterate i from 0 to 2^n - 1 and emit that.

stress.description: random width across the allowed range
stress.note: n is the only argument; the full range stays well within time limits
